ddls:
CREATE TABLE "motors" (
  "motor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each motor */,
  "motor_name" TEXT /* Name of the motor */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the motor is installed */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the motor was installed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the motor (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the motor */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the motor */,
  "warranty_expiration" TEXT /* Warranty expiration date */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Planned date for the next maintenance */,
  "operational_hours" INTEGER /* Total operational hours of the motor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("motor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance data entry */,
  "motor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the motor being monitored */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the recorded data point */,
  "rpm" REAL /* Revolutions per minute (RPM) of the motor */,
  "motor_voltage" REAL /* Voltage supplied to the motor */,
  "motor_current" REAL /* Current flowing through the motor */,
  "motor_temp" REAL /* Temperature of the motor */,
  "inlet_temp" REAL /* Temperature of the inlet */,
  "power_consumption" REAL /* Power consumption of the motor */,
  "efficiency" REAL /* Efficiency of the motor */,
  "vibration_level" REAL /* Vibration level of the motor */,
  "airflow" REAL /* Airflow around the motor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_motor_id FOREIGN KEY ("motor_id") REFERENCES motors ("motor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "motor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the motor associated with the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., voltage, current, temperature) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" TEXT /* Name of the user who resolved the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_motor_id FOREIGN KEY ("motor_id") REFERENCES motors ("motor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "motor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the motor serviced */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date when the maintenance was performed */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance performed */,
  "performed_by" TEXT /* Name of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance performed (e.g., routine, emergency) */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the maintenance in minutes */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the maintenance */,
  "parts_replaced" TEXT /* List of parts replaced during the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_motor_id FOREIGN KEY ("motor_id") REFERENCES motors ("motor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, technician, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login timestamp of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Description of permissions associated with that role */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* Name of the user who created the role */,
  "created_on" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was created */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* Name of the user who last updated the role */,
  "updated_on" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration setting */,
  "motor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the motor associated with the configuration */,
  "parameter" TEXT /* Parameter being configured (e.g., voltage, temperature) */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for alert generation */,
  "value_type" TEXT /* Type of value (e.g., max, min) */,
  "last_modified_by" TEXT /* Name of the user who last modified the configuration */,
  "last_modified_on" TEXT /* Timestamp when the configuration was last modified */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the configuration is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_motor_id FOREIGN KEY ("motor_id") REFERENCES motors ("motor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access event */,
  "accessed_table" TEXT /* Table being accessed (e.g., performance_data, alerts) */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, update) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "success" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the access attempt was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
